component_identification.content: Each player gets a unique villain board with four locations, a villain mover, fate deck, villain deck, reference card, and power tokens. Card types include allies, items, effects, conditions, heroes, and sometimes unique objective pieces. Every villain has a different win condition and deck identity.

core_game_loop.content: On your turn, move your villain to a different location, then perform all available actions at that location in any order. Common actions are gain power, play cards, activate cards, defeat heroes, discard, and fate an opponent. Players race to satisfy their own villain objective while slowing others with fate cards.

detailed_rules.content: Location choice is mandatory each turn and cannot repeat your current location. Power is the main currency used to play cards from hand. Allies and items are played to your realm locations and can cover action spaces if heroes oppose them. To remove heroes, allies at that location must have enough strength (plus modifiers) to defeat them. Fate action targets one opponent, revealing cards from that opponent’s fate deck to place heroes/effects that obstruct progress. Many villains rely on sequencing: generating power engines, finding key cards, and timing effects/conditions. Hand management matters because discard is often limited by location access. Read your villain guide carefully; setup and objective wording can alter normal assumptions (for example, Jafar needing the Magic Lamp and moving Genie to the Sultanate, Ursula managing contracts, etc.).

scoring_and_endgame.content: First player to meet their villain-specific objective at the required game timing wins immediately (unless a card says to check at start/end of turn). No point totals are used; victory is binary and asymmetric.

beginner_strategy.content: Learn one villain deeply before rotating often; matchup knowledge is huge. Prioritize reliable power generation early so your later turns are flexible. Watch opponents’ apparent win thresholds and use fate proactively before they are one turn away. Keep your hand cycling by discarding situational dead cards. When fating, choose the target most likely to win soon rather than spreading disruption evenly.
